items.text: Dana Whitfield, product manager at a healthcare data company in Portland
Mid-quarter roadmap review scheduled for Thursday 2025-02-06; working through prioritization between interoperability work and analytics dashboard for exec recommendation
items.archivedReason: Mid-quarter roadmap review from 2025-02-06 is now 11 weeks past; work prioritization decision is stale current-state with no ongoing thread or emotional significance

items.text: Sam, partner of six years; described as 'great' support during difficult time
Marcus, brother, lives in Denver; close relationship but haven't seen each other in over a year, currently only phone contact
Wants to see brother Marcus in person this year after over a year of phone-only contact; described as 'a real intention'

supersededBy.text: Plans to visit Marcus in Denver before summer 2025 to meet niece Nina; Marcus offered 'the guest room is yours'; Dana described this as 'obligation, the good kind' and 'a thing I owe now, and I want to owe it' (as of 2025-03-09)

items.text: 2025-02-03: Lost her mother recently; described winter as 'heavy' and said the loss 'colors everything'; did not want to discuss details this session
Priya, closest friend from grad school; ceramicist and freelance designer; Dana and Priya signed up for a Tuesday night pottery class together starting 2025-02-12
Attending Tuesday night pottery class with friend Priya (started 2025-02-12); first standing regular activity they've had together in years

2025-02-12: 'My mom had an old wooden sailboat, a 22-footer called the Dorothy Jean — named after my grandmother. It's been sitting under a tarp in Priya's dad's barn out near Scappoose since mom got sick. I went out Sunday, pulled the tarp off, and just... started. Sanded one plank. I've decided I'm going to restore her. Slowly. No deadline.' Said 'Mom taught me to sail on that boat' and 'working with my hands on something of hers felt like the first right thing in months.'
Restoring mother's wooden sailboat (the Dorothy Jean, a 22-footer named after her grandmother) stored in Priya's dad's barn near Scappoose; began work 2025-02-10, taking it slowly with no deadline

[as of 2025-02-24] Training for Lakefront Marathon October 2025; structured plan with four runs weekly including Saturday long runs; completed eight miles 2025-02-22; first marathon (previously ran 5Ks and one half marathon ~four years ago)
items.compressedFrom: [as of 2025-02-24] Training for Lakefront Marathon in October 2025; following a structured plan with four runs per week including a long run every Saturday morning; completed eight miles on 2025-02-22; first marathon (previously ran 5Ks and one half marathon about four years ago)

items.text: 2025-02-24: On signing up for the marathon: 'this winter I needed something with a long arc, something that's just mine. Twenty-six point two felt right.' Said 'It's the first time since mom that I've been excited about something in the future instead of dreading it.'

2025-03-09: Marcus and Elena had their baby — a girl, Nina, born March 6th. Marcus called from the hospital hallway at 2am 'just to say "she's here."' Dana said 'We haven't talked like that — voice shaking, no agenda — in years' and 'I cried at my desk' when she saw the photo of Nina in a little knit hat.
Nina, niece, daughter of Marcus and Elena; born 2025-03-06 (as of 2025-03-09)
Elena, Marcus's partner; mother of Nina (as of 2025-03-09)

Plans to visit Marcus in Denver before summer 2025 to meet niece Nina; Marcus offered 'the guest room is yours'; Dana described this as 'obligation, the good kind' and 'a thing I owe now, and I want to owe it' (as of 2025-03-09)

2025-03-14: Scattered her mother's ashes at Beacon Rock in the Columbia River Gorge. Went alone. Said 'Beacon Rock was ours — she used to take me there when I was a kid, and on the way up she taught me to read cloud shapes. Not just "that one's a rabbit" — she had this whole taxonomy, what a mare's tail sky meant, when the anvil clouds meant get off the water. I stood at the top and the sky was doing exactly the kind of high thin herringbone she loved, and I let her go into the wind.' Picked up a fir cone from the trail on the way down; it's on the windowsill above her kitchen sink. Sam had soup waiting when she got home; slept eleven hours. (2025-03-18)

[as of 2025-04-01] Sam received job offer as Perception team lead at robotics startup in Austin, Texas; described as 'genuinely his dream problem space' and 'strong'; decision deadline early May 2025; Dana and Sam considering relocation together
items.compressedFrom: [as of 2025-04-01] Sam received job offer as Perception team lead at robotics startup in Austin, Texas; offer described as 'genuinely his dream problem space' and 'strong'; decision deadline early May 2025; Dana and Sam considering whether to relocate together

supersededBy.text: Sam accepted Perception team lead role at robotics startup in Austin, Texas, with remote work arrangement from Portland; starts June 2025; will travel to Austin approximately one week every couple months

items.text: Deciding whether to move to Austin with Sam for his job offer vs. staying in Portland where her healing process is geographically rooted ('This geography is load-bearing for me right now'); decision needed by early May 2025; described as 'torn in a way that surprises me'; Sam said he won't take it if it costs Dana this year of healing

supersededBy.text: Resolved 2025-05-11: Staying in Portland. Sam negotiated remote work arrangement with Austin robotics startup (Perception team lead role); will work from Portland with occasional travel to Austin (approximately one week every couple months); starts June 2025. Sam's offer accepted by company. Dana described relief at not having to leave 'load-bearing geography' this year.

items.text: 2025-04-01: On the Austin decision: 'My whole life fabric is here. Priya. The pottery class. The barn. The boat — I can't exactly trailer a half-restored 22-foot sailboat to Texas, and I'm not done with what that boat is doing for me. And it feels absurd to weigh a boat against Sam's dream job, but it's not really the boat, you know?' and 'Mom's here. Not literally anymore — but the Gorge, Beacon Rock, the barn. This geography is load-bearing for me right now.'
[as of 2025-04-01] Marathon training week six; completed twelve miles on Saturday 2025-03-29

supersededBy.text: Marathon training halted; sprained ankle badly on 2025-04-13 during trail run in Forest Park (grade-two sprain, possibly worse); in walking boot, orthopedist appointment 2025-04-18; withdrew from Lakefront Marathon October 2025; doctor advised eight to ten weeks before running again

items.text: Marathon training halted; sprained ankle badly on 2025-04-13 during trail run in Forest Park (grade-two sprain, possibly worse); in walking boot, orthopedist appointment 2025-04-18; withdrew from Lakefront Marathon October 2025; doctor advised eight to ten weeks before running again

2025-04-14: On withdrawing from the marathon after ankle injury: 'That training arc was the first future-shaped thing I'd built since mom.' and 'I withdrew my registration this morning so I couldn't waffle about it. It's off. I'm not doing the thing where I pretend it might still happen.' Sam pointed out the fitness isn't lost and there's always next year; Dana said 'he's right, and I don't want to hear it yet.'

Conflict as of 2025-04-27: Priya asked to borrow $8,000 for ceramics studio lease deposit and buildout; Dana declined immediately citing uncertain finances (Austin decision pending) and policy against mixing money and friendship (informed by past experience lending to an ex); delivery was abrupt and hurt the friendship; Priya responded with 'very quiet and then very polite' withdrawal; have skipped pottery class twice in nearly two weeks since; Dana describes it as 'too raw' to repair yet
[as of 2025-04-27] Walking boot removal scheduled for Thursday 2025-04-30; physical therapy to begin after boot removal; orthopedist reports ankle healing on schedule
items.archivedReason: Both items are current-state snapshots from same session (2025-04-27) with closely related timeframes; merging reduces redundant date stamps while preserving all facts

items.text: [as of 2025-04-27] Walking boot removal scheduled for Thursday 2025-04-30; physical therapy to begin after; ankle healing on schedule; Sam's Austin job decision deadline approximately one week away (early May 2025)

supersededBy.text: [as of 2025-05-11] Out of walking boot; attending physical therapy twice weekly; walking normally-ish; ankle recovering

items.text: Sam accepted Perception team lead role at robotics startup in Austin, Texas, with remote work arrangement from Portland; starts June 2025; will travel to Austin approximately one week every couple months
[as of 2025-05-11] Out of walking boot; attending physical therapy twice weekly; walking normally-ish; ankle recovering

supersededBy.text: [as of 2025-05-24] Cleared by physical therapist for light jogging (twenty minutes, flat ground, every other day); completed first jog 2025-05-24 morning along esplanade; ankle recovering; goal is pain-free running by fall without re-injury

items.text: 2025-05-11: On Sam's remote work arrangement being approved: 'When he told me they'd agreed, I cried a little, which surprised me. I hadn't let myself feel how scared I was of leaving this year — the barn, the boat, Beacon Rock, all of it. And I'd been quietly rehearsing how to be graceful about going. Not needing the rehearsal anymore was the relief.' (2025-05-11)
[as of 2025-05-11] Boat restoration progress: starboard side sanded, started re-caulking hull seams on the Dorothy Jean

[as of 2025-05-24] Cleared by physical therapist for light jogging (twenty minutes, flat ground, every other day); completed first jog 2025-05-24 morning along esplanade; ankle recovering; goal is pain-free running by fall without re-injury
[as of 2025-05-24] Sam built out spare room as home office (three monitors, foam panels) for remote Austin role; starts in couple weeks; described as nervous-excited

entries.note: Session 2025-04-27 shows significant strain: loan request refused, two pottery classes skipped, communication breakdown. Relationship currently damaged but Dana expresses intent to repair ('not yet, though').
Session 2025-05-11: Dana notes Nina is two months old and she still hasn't visited Denver; says it's 'starting to itch at me' — awareness of unfulfilled intention but no concrete plan yet

2025-05-24: Dana states 'I've made peace with' the marathon being off this year and describes being 'weirdly okay with it now'; attributes shift to 'The ankle taught me something about not white-knuckling timelines.' Contrast with sal-23 (2025-04-14) where withdrawal felt like loss of 'the first future-shaped thing I'd built since mom' and she 'didn't want to hear' reassurance. Appears to be genuine acceptance rather than resignation.
